source_jobs.title: Culinary Assurance Supervisor, 2nd Shift
source_jobs.description:
The Culinary Assurance Supervisor serves as a key leader accountable for performance and integrity of food safety, quality and culinary assurance. The ideal candidate will have a passion for delivering safe, quality and delicious food to the Kevin’s Natural Foods consumer. This role ensures day-to-day compliance and product quality. This role will lead others to deliver safe and delicious excellence. If you don’t love adapting to the changes of a fast-growing business, this opportunity is not for you. The ideal candidate will be efficient at self-managing and looking for a long-term commitment to grow within a dynamic environment.



This is a 2nd Shift position with the hours of 3:00PM-11:30PM, Monday-Friday, and working Saturdays as needed.



What You’ll Do:

· Collaborate with Operations, Sanitation and leadership to drive ownership of food safety, quality, and culinary standards on the production floor

· Work closely with cross-functional teams to ensure quality and culinary standards are integrated into the entire production process

· Develop, recommend and monitor corrective actions when deficiencies in food safety or quality are identified

· Supervise and mentor the Q&FS teams and beyond through training, coaching and performance management

· Provide ongoing support for hourly team members across facilities

· Implement food safety, quality assurance and culinary programs to ensure compliance with regulatory requirements and finished product standards

· Plan, conduct and monitor inspections and testing of materials and products to ensure safety and culinary quality

· Generate reports describing activities, trends and expenditures; communicate findings and action plan to necessary/appropriate parties; to include lab data technical analysis

· Ensure adherence to quality programs, including HACCP and GMPs

· Drive continuous improvement initiatives to enhance the food safety culture and reduce risk across facilities

· Develop and execute training programs to support the company’s food safety, quality and culinary objectives

· Ensure the prompt and accurate implementation of new regulations at facilities and with suppliers

· Represent the Q&FS team on cross-functional teams and collaborate on company-wide initiatives

· Other duties as assigned



What You’ll Need:

· Passion for delivering safe, quality and delicious food to the consumer.

· At least 3-4 years experience in a management role (w/ direct reports) in a commercial food environment (food manufacturing, or multi-unit food operator, or similar)

· Quality Assurance or Food Safety experience in a food manufacturing plant strongly desired

· Restaurant cooking experience strongly desired

· USDA experience a plus

· Knowledge of food safety principes, including HACCP, GMPs and the willingness to learn more

· Proficient in Microsoft Office, Outlook, Excel, and SAP.

· Self-motivated with proven leadership skills.

· Thrive in a start-up environment.

· Bilingual - English/Spanish is a plus.

· Must be able to work in a team environment.

· Must have a positive attitude.

· Must have good written and oral communication skills.

· Ability to work in fast-paced, effectively under pressure, and deadline-oriented environment.

· Flexible availability: we work night and weekend shifts.

· Lifting of up to 50 pounds

· Comfortable working in envinonments with varying temperatures

· Ability to handle chemicals and solvents according to MSDS guidelines

· Degree is strongly desired

The team at Kevin’s is smart, humble, and passionate and we value a work environment that fosters personal development and opportunities to move within our small, but quickly growing organization.

source_jobs.title: Material Planner
source_jobs.description:
The Material Planner will be responsible for managing raw material & packaging inventory and ensure material availability is consistent with factory production schedules/requirements. Further responsibilities include managing supplier relationships, using ERP systems to manage purchase orders and on-time delivery, participation in continuous improvement programs, coordinating with internal departments, and managing stakeholder relations. This individual will liaise with category buyers, master scheduler, factory operations/production teams. We are looking for individuals who thrive in fast-paced environments. The ideal candidate will be hard working, punctual, dependable, self-motivated, self-managed, organized, flexible, and looking for a long-term commitment to grow within a growing company.



What You’ll Do:

· Manage and maintain purchase orders for direct sourced raw materials and packaging using MRP/SAP based systems or processes.

· Work with suppliers on a daily basis and manage Supplier Delivery Performance (On Time In Full) KPIs - Need to be extremely proactive with a high sense of urgency on supplier follow ups for purchase order delivery dates and ordered quantities, this also involves tracking shipments via ASNs (Advanced Shipping Notices) for ground and ocean freight to ensure it is delivered and received on time at the right warehouse.

· Support factory operations and production team, buyers and purchasing activities, mitigate supply issues and inventory replenishment/replacement due to inventory adjustments or non-conforming material, establish root cause and corrective action.

· Manages dynamic forecasts and manages replenishment planning for materials to ensure specified customer service levels are met.

· Communicate daily issues, including specification changes, deviations, late deliveries, etc. with factory production team and category buyers.

· Manage Supplier Lead Times, MOQs and Transit times.

· Knowledge of relationship management techniques; ability to establish, develop and maintain healthy working relationships with suppliers, internal cross functional teams, and peers.

· Organizes the internal flow of materials within the manufacturing unit/factory and the transferring of materials to and from the inventory or warehouse.

· Expedite raw materials and packaging materials from approved suppliers as needed with consideration for cost and value.

· Escalate challenging supply issues to category buyers and related stakeholders in a timely manner after exhaustion of efforts.

· Participate and implement continuous improvement programs to reduce improve quality, lead-time, and reduce costs.

· Participate in continuous improvement programs and activities, develop and implement cost-saving strategies without compromising quality or service.

· Extensive experience in Decision Making and Critical Thinking: Knowledge of the decision-making process and associated tools and techniques; ability to accurately analyze situations and reach productive decisions based on informed judgment.

· Extensive experience in Problem Solving: Knowledge of approaches, tools, techniques for recognizing, anticipating, and resolving organizational, operational or process problems; ability to apply knowledge of problem solving appropriately to diverse situations, issues, and considerations for systematically managing distribution of inventory for multiple plants or warehouses; ability to apply related knowledge and experience in real practice

· Other duties as assigned or necessary.





What You’ll Need:



· Bachelor’s degree in business related field or similar.

· Minimum 1-2 years’ experience in material planning or scheduling, inventory management and working with vendors/suppliers.

source_jobs.title: S&OP Implementation Lead
source_jobs.description:
Kevin’s Natural Foods is growing fast! We’re looking for an S&OP Implementation Lead with the strategic vision and operational know-how to help build the next evolution of our integrated planning capability. This role is perfect for someone who thrives in a hands‑on, entrepreneurial environment and wants to make a meaningful impact on how we plan, align, and execute. This hybrid role will shape the future of planning at one of the fastest‑growing clean‑label food brands in the heart of San Joaquin! As our S&OP Implementation Lead you will co‑design the roadmap for S&OP implementation, engage senior leaders to create top‑down alignment, and guide the organization through cultural and process transformation. You’ll help modernize tools and systems, establish governance, and lead the change efforts needed to embed a disciplined, data‑driven planning cadence.



What You’ll Do:

Build & Lead the S&OP Roadmap

 - Co‑develop and maintain the multi‑year S&OP implementation roadmap, including vision, maturity milestones, capability readiness, and an organizational change adoption strategy.

 - Translate business objectives into an actionable planning model connecting demand, supply, inventory, capacity, and financial processes.

Engage Leadership & Drive Alignment

 - Partner directly with senior leaders to establish S&OP as a shared enterprise priority and a top-down cultural expectation.

 - Facilitate worksops, alignment sessions, and training to prepare and guide senior leaders through S&OP roles, expectations, and decision‑rights necessary for an effective operating cadence.

 - Serve as the primary change agent and communicator for S&OP transformation.

Assess & Prepare the Organization

 - Evaluate current processes, planning behaviors, and cross‑functional readiness.

 - Build education, coaching, and support models that strengthen planning discipline and collaboration.

 - Shape a culture grounded in transparency, data fluency, and the operational excellence.

 - Assess existing planning tools, system capabilities, data quality, and reporting workflows.

 - Recommend future-state tool enhancements or potential investments to support more advanced S&OP maturity.

 - Partner with IT, Supply Chain, Finance, and Commercial teams to ensure business requirements are clear and aligned.

Develop Governance & Lead Change Adoption

 - Design and launch S&OP governance frameworks, including roles, responsibilities, decision‑rights, escalation paths, and KPI structures.

 - Define the standard meeting cadence (Demand Review, Supply Review, Pre‑S&OP, Executive S&OP) and ensure process discipline.

 - Develop and execute change‑management plans that address communication, training, stakeholder management, and sustained adoption.

 - Build playbooks, process documentation, training materials, and readiness assessments.

 - Identify risks to adoption and proactively remove barriers to progress.

 - Other duties as assigned or necessary.



What You’ll Need:

 - 5-8 years of progressive experience in Supply Chain, S&OP, Integrated Business Planning, or related transformation roles within food, beverage, or CPG manufacturing.

 - Experience with customer-centric organizational culture and short shelf-life products strongly preferred.

 - Exceptional communication, facilitation, and relationship-building skills.

 - Demonstrated expertise in process design, change management, and continuous improvement methodologies.

 - Working knowledge of planning tools (e.g. ERP, demand planning platforms) and core S&OP analytics.

 - Coaching mentality with ability to translate complexity into simple, actionable concepts.

 - A collaborative, low‑ego approach with the ability to influence without authority.

source_jobs.title: Cost Accountant Clerk
source_jobs.description:
Kevin’s Natural Foods is on the hunt for a Cost Accountant Clerk who is organized, resourceful, humble, and hungry. The individual is expected to be detailed oriented, knowledgeable on statistics, have strong problem-solving skills, work comfortably under pressure, and deliver on tight deadlines A successful Cost Accountant Clerk should be confident, professional, and meticulous with a keen interest in the cost-efficient operations of the organization. Top candidates will have outstanding presentation and report-writing skills, with the ability to work on their own initiative and as part of a team.



What You’ll Do:

· Collecting and analyzing data about operational costs

· Setting standard costs of manufactured goods

· Developing and analyzing cost standards

· Issue Daily, Weekly, Monthly, Quarterly and Annual KPI Reports

· Measure and issue daily information on labor and yield

· Analyze operational results daily and weekly to aid Operational Managers control costs.

· Develop Costing for new products and/or product extensions.

· Develop, analyze and present What If scenarios to help Operations improve processes

· Carrying out cost-volume-profit (CVP) analysis to establish profit margins

· Establishing costs of business processes such as administration, labor, shipping, etc.

· Review our Bill of Materials (BoM) vs Actuals for opportunities and accuracy.

· Other duties as assigned.



What You’ll Need:

· Bachelors degree in accounting or related field is preferred

· 2 or more years of Cost accountant or equivalent field experience

· Advanced knowledge of mathematics and statistics

source_jobs.title: Operations Manager
source_jobs.description:
The Operations Manager is responsible for overseeing all aspects of the production process within a food manufacturing facility. This role ensures the implementation of effective processes and systems to achieve safety, quality, production, and cost objectives. Key responsibilities include driving operational excellence, maintaining regulatory compliance, optimizing labor and inventory, and leading team development. The Operations Manager plays a critical role in ensuring product quality, food safety, and continuous improvement across all manufacturing operations.



What You’ll Do:

· Lead and develop teams to maximize performance, efficiency, service levels, and workplace safety.

· Ensure all products meet established quality standards and specifications.

· Establish, implement, and enforce policies and procedures, including compliance with regulatory requirements (USDA, FDA), HACCP, and GMPs.

· Ensure accurate and timely completion of all QA and production documentation.

· Develop and manage weekly production schedules aligned with sales forecasts.

· Allocate production labor effectively to meet operational and productivity targets.

· Implement systems to monitor incoming raw material quality and ensure supplier compliance with company standards.

· Drive continuous improvement initiatives through analysis of production metrics and performance data.

· Ensure compliance with environmental, health, and safety regulations, including energy conservation programs.

· Collaborate cross-functionally with Maintenance, Procurement, R&D, Sales, and Marketing to support operational improvements and growth initiatives.

· Develop, implement, and maintain standard operating procedures (SOPs) for existing and new products.

· Drive cost reduction initiatives and improve product yield and operational efficiency.

· Coach and develop front-line supervisors and team members to resolve operational challenges and achieve performance goals.

· Monitor equipment performance, reliability, and maintenance of company assets.

· Other duties as assigned or necessary



What You’ll Need:

· Minimum of 5 years of leadership experience in a food manufacturing environment, preferably producing sauces, starches, or fully cooked proteins.

· Proven experience collaborating cross-functionally with Quality Assurance, Operations, Maintenance, Materials, and Procurement teams.

· HACCP certification required.

· Strong leadership, coaching, facilitation, presentation, and team-building skills.

· Demonstrated ability to implement and sustain process improvements and operational controls.

source_jobs.title: Sales/CPFR Analyst - Walmart
source_jobs.description:
Kevin’s Natural Foods is seeking a highly analytical and execution-driven Sales/CPFR Analyst – Walmart to support profitable sales growth and strengthen our partnership with Walmart.In this role, you will own forecasting, replenishment planning, and day-to-day CPFR execution for the Walmart account. You will work closely with the National Account Manager and cross-functional teams to drive strong in-stock performance, optimize inventory flow, and ensure seamless supply chain execution. You will leverage Walmart’s Scintilla platform along with internal data to analyze performance, proactively manage risks, and deliver actionable insights across sales, inventory, and operational metrics. This is a high-impact role for a self-starter who thrives in a fast-paced, data-intensive environment and takes full ownership of execution.



What You’ll Do:

· Own demand forecasting and replenishment planning for the Walmart business, driving forecast accuracy and alignment with internal and customer expectations.

· Monitor in-stock performance, inventory levels, and replenishment trends, proactively identifying risks and executing action plans to maintain strong shelf availability.

· Leverage Scintilla and internal data to analyze performance, identify opportunities, and support fact-based decision making.

· Partner closely with supply chain, demand planning, and replenishment teams to ensure alignment across forecasts, production, and inventory flow.

· Manage and track key Walmart operational metrics, including OTIF, ASN accuracy, fill rates, and inventory health.

· Identify and resolve issues related to order management, replenishment, inventory discrepancies, and supply chain execution.

· Own item setup, maintenance, and lifecycle management, ensuring accuracy across Walmart systems.

· Prepare weekly and monthly performance reports, dashboards, and business updates for the National Account Manager and internal stakeholders.

· Serve as a key analytical and operational partner to the National Account Manager, ensuring strong execution across all Walmart initiatives.

· Identify opportunities to improve processes, reporting, and overall efficiency within the Walmart business.

· Collaborate cross functionally to contest invalid fines and fees.

· Other duties as assigned or necessary.



Qualifications:

· Bachelor’s degree in business administration, Analytics, Finance, Supply Chain, or a related field.

· 3-7 years of progressive experience in CPG sales analytics, supply chain, CPFR, or customer business analytics, with direct Walmart experience required.

· Strong hands-on experience with Walmart’s Scintilla platform and related tools.

· Proven ability to manage forecasting, replenishment, and in-stock performance within a retail CPG environment.

· Strong understanding of Walmart operational processes, including OTIF, ASN requirements, and supply chain execution.

· Analytical mindset with the ability to translate data into actionable insights and drive execution.

· Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel; experience building dashboards and performance reporting tools.

· Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.

· Effective communication skills, with the ability to collaborate cross-functionally and influence outcomes.

· Experience in refrigerated, frozen, or perishable categories preferred.

· Experience in a high-growth CPG company preferred.

· Based in Northwest Arkansas (Bentonville / Rogers area) with minimal travel required.

source_jobs.title: Senior Warehouse Manager
source_jobs.description:
The Senior Warehouse Manager is a strategic leader responsible for overseeing end-to-end warehouse and distribution operations across multiple facilities. This role brings deep expertise in food storage, inventory control, and logistics, while driving operational excellence, scalability, and continuous improvement. This leader ensures best-in-class execution across inventory management (FIFO/FEFO), warehouse planning, labor optimization, and distribution, while maintaining strict compliance with all food safety, quality, environmental, and occupational health standards, including GMP, HACCP, SSOP, and OSHA regulations. They play a critical role in building high-performing teams, optimizing systems and processes, and supporting the company’s rapid growth. We are seeking a highly driven, hands-on leader who thrives in fast-paced environments, brings a strong sense of ownership, and is passionate about developing people, improving systems, and delivering results.



What You’ll Do:

· Lead and oversee warehouse operations across multiple facilities, ensuring alignment with company goals and growth initiatives

· Develop and execute warehouse strategies to improve throughput, accuracy, service levels, and cost efficiency

· Build, coach, and develop high-performing exempt and non-exempt teams; establish strong leadership bench strength

· Partner cross-functionally with Production, QA/FSQA, Procurement, and Logistics to ensure seamless operations

· Drive best-in-class inventory management practices, ensuring strict adherence to FIFO/FEFO and inventory accuracy targets

· Establish, monitor, and continuously improve KPIs (inventory accuracy, fill rate, labor productivity, on-time shipping, etc.)

· Lead warehouse planning, slotting optimization, and capacity management to support business growth

· Oversee inbound, storage, staging, and outbound operations to ensure efficiency, accuracy, and compliance

· Implement and enhance SOPs and processes to improve operational performance and scalability

· Leverage ERP/WMS systems to improve visibility, reporting, and decision-making

· Identify and lead continuous improvement initiatives, including automation, process standardization, and waste reduction

· Ensure accurate and timely inventory transactions, cycle counts, and reporting

· Ensure full compliance with all regulatory and company standards, including GMP, HACCP, SSOP, OSHA, and environmental requirements

· Partner closely with FSQA to uphold product quality and food safety standards across all warehouse operations

· Maintain audit readiness at all times and lead responses to internal and external audits

· Ensure accurate receiving, storage, and shipment of products, including proper documentation and verification processes

· Oversee inventory control procedures, including cycle counts and reconciliation

· Coordinate intercompany transfers across facilities

· Support production teams by ensuring timely staging and availability of materials

· Maintain warehouse conditions, including sanitation, organization, and environmental controls

· Champion a culture of safety, accountability, and continuous improvement

· Ensure adherence to all safety programs and proactively reduce workplace incidents

· Lead training programs on GMPs, safety procedures, and operational best practices

· Other duties as assigned or necessary.



What You’ll Need:

· Bachelor’s degree preferred

· 7+ years of progressive warehouse/logistics experience, including leadership across multiple locations

· Strong background in food manufacturing or fresh/perishable goods highly preferred

· Deep understanding of GMP, HACCP, SSOP, OSHA, and food safety standards

· Proven experience with ERP/WMS systems and data-driven decision making

source_jobs.title: Supplier Quality Analyst
source_jobs.description:
The Supplier Quality Assurance (SQA) Analyst is responsible for ensuring the quality and food safety of raw materials and packaging materials supplied to Kevin’s Natural Foods. This role safeguards product integrity by leading supplier qualification, risk assessments, audits, and continuous improvement initiatives while driving supplier performance and maintaining inbound material quality. The SQA Analyst serves as a key cross-functional partner across Procurement, R&D, Supply Chain, Operations, and Quality teams to establish and maintain supplier quality programs, support new product introductions, and ensure compliance with applicable quality and food safety standards.



What You’ll Do:



· Own supplier qualification, approval, onboarding, and ongoing performance management to ensure compliance with quality and food safety requirements.

· Establish and maintain supplier quality processes, policies, metrics, and technical documentation to support material quality and continuous improvement.

· Perform Quality and Food Safety (Q&FS) risk assessments for low-, medium-, and high-risk raw materials and packaging, ensuring microbiological, chemical, and physical risks are identified and translated into material specifications and verification programs.

· Collect and review supplier documentation, certifications, and technical information to support material risk assessments and evaluate supplier controls used to manage identified risks.

· Partner with Procurement and suppliers to review, align, and maintain material specifications, risk assessments, and quality expectations.

· Collaborate with Quality and Food Safety teams to establish inbound verification programs and material testing requirements based on risk assessments.

· Partner with Procurement, R&D, and Operations to support ingredient and packaging changes, innovation projects, and new product launches.

· Monitor and improve inbound raw and packaging material quality through KPIs, supplier performance metrics, verification activities, and continuous improvement initiatives.

· Conduct supplier audits, initial assessments, and ongoing evaluations of low-, medium-, and high-risk suppliers to verify the effectiveness of quality systems, food safety controls, and regulatory compliance.

· Represent Kevin’s Natural Foods at supplier facilities and ensure suppliers maintain approved status and defined verification plans.

· Lead investigations related to raw material quality issues, non-conformances, and deviations using root cause analysis to determine disposition and drive effective corrective and preventive actions (CAPA).

· Establish and maintain processes for supplier-related non-conformance investigations in collaboration with Quality and Food Safety teams.

· Manage change control activities impacting material quality and food safety and maintain supplier risk assessments and approved supplier status.

· Drive supplier development initiatives and leverage performance trends and data to improve supplier capability, support sourcing strategies, and provide recommendations to management.

· Build and maintain strong relationships with suppliers and cross-functional stakeholders to ensure consistent product quality and business continuity.

· Perform other duties as assigned.





What You'll Need:



· Bachelor’s degree in food science, Chemistry, Microbiology, or a related field preferred.

· 3–5 years of experience in Quality Assurance, Food Safety, Supplier Quality, or related technical functions within the food industry.

· Experience managing supplier quality programs, supplier relationships, and material quality systems.

· Strong background auditing supplier facilities and Quality Management Systems.

· Strong understanding of Quality and Food Safety standards, including HACCP, GMP, FSSC 22000, and ISO 22000.

· Experience performing ingredient and material risk assessments and developing verification programs.

source_jobs.title: Senior Customer Care and Compliance Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
The Senior Customer Care & Compliance Coordinator is responsible for supporting and executing customer compliance programs, researching and resolving non‑trade deductions, and improving financial recovery from CPG retail partners. This role partners closely with Sales, Accounts Receivable, Logistics, Operations, and Customer Care to ensure adherence to customer requirements and prevent repeat deductions. In addition to compliance, the Coordinator supports customer care and logistics reporting, cost‑savings identification, and process documentation. The role builds strong operational, analytical, and cross‑functional skills, with increasing opportunities to use AI tools to streamline manual tasks and enhance reporting accuracy.



What You’ll Do:

Deductions, Chargebacks & Compliance

 - Support the full manual lifecycle of customer deductions and chargebacks, including tracking, research, dispute submission, and resolution.

 - Reduce repeat non‑trade deductions by identifying root causes and coordinating corrective actions with Sales, AR, Logistics, Operations, and carriers.

 - Maintain accurate documentation of disputes, recoveries, and savings for Finance and leadership visibility.

 - Improve timeliness and accuracy of dispute handling, moving toward same‑cycle or real‑time resolution.

 - Ensure adherence to customer routing guides, compliance requirements, and service expectations.

Customer Care & Logistics Reporting

 - Conduct weekly and monthly reporting for chargebacks, fines, shortages, OTIF, freight performance, and corrective actions.

 - Maintain accuracy and consistency of all reporting within the current manual environment.

 - Track and analyze key KPIs including on‑time delivery, shortages, freight cost, and accessorial charges.

 - Standardize reporting outputs to ensure clarity, repeatability, and leadership usability.

 - Partner with Finance to define reporting requirements and support future automation readiness.

Cost Savings & Performance Improvement

 - Identify cost‑savings opportunities across chargebacks, fines, pallet inefficiencies, freight costs, and customer care processes.

 - Track and validate realized savings with supporting documentation.

 - Provide insights and recommendations that improve service performance and reduce financial exposure.

 - Support leadership updates with consistent savings reporting and operational visibility.

Process Documentation & Scalability

 - Develop and maintain clear work instructions for deduction management, reporting, and compliance workflows.

 - Define escalation paths and decision points for complex dispute scenarios.

 - Maintain centralized documentation and update processes as they evolve.

 - Build documentation that supports future transition from manual workflows to automated tools.

Cross‑Functional Collaboration & Systems

 - Partner closely with Sales, Accounts Receivable, Logistics, Operations, and Customer Care to resolve issues and prevent repeat deductions.

 - Support operational decision‑making with insights into service performance, cost drivers, and compliance trends.

 - Participate in system enhancements involving SAP, CRM, or EDI that impact compliance or reporting.

 - Use AI tools to automate recurring tasks, streamline reporting, and improve data accuracy as capabilities expand.

 - Other duties as assigned or necessary.



What You’ll Need

 - High school diploma or equivalent; additional training or coursework is a plus.

 - 2–4 years of experience in deductions, chargebacks, compliance, customer service operations, AR, or supply chain.

 - Strong investigative and analytical skills with the ability to interpret documentation and identify root causes.

 - Experience with SAP, CRM, or order management systems; familiarity with retailer portals is a plus.

 - Excellent communication and cross‑functional collaboration skills.

 - Ability to manage multiple deadlines and exercise sound judgment.

 - Interest in developing advanc

source_jobs.title: Quality Assurance Technician 2nd Shift
source_jobs.description:

The ideal candidate will be hard working, punctual, dependable, self-motivated, self-managed, organized, flexible, and looking for a long-term commitment to grow within a growing company.

 - Work closely with production staff to maintain and education in the areas of sanitation, hygiene, GMP’s and other food safety guidelines.

 - Collect product samples for product development, lab retains, and the laboratory when directed.

 - Perform inspections on products, equipment, and machinery.

 - 1-3 years’ experience in a food processing environment.

 - Proficient computer skills and Microsoft Office Applications (Outlook, Word, and Excel)

 - Excellent interpersonal, verbal, and written communication and problem-solving skills

source_jobs.title: Journeyman Electrician
source_jobs.description:
The Journeyman Electrician is responsible for installing, maintaining, troubleshooting, and repairing electrical systems and industrial equipment within a fast-paced frozen food manufacturing facility. This role plays a key part in supporting production uptime, food safety, and operational efficiency by ensuring electrical systems and controls operate safely and reliably. The ideal candidate brings extensive hands-on electrical maintenance experience within a manufacturing environment and is comfortable working independently in a high-volume operation.



What You’ll Do:

· Troubleshoot, repair, and maintain industrial electrical systems, production equipment, motors, drives, control panels, and automation systems.

· Perform preventative maintenance and inspections on facility electrical systems and manufacturing equipment.

· Diagnose electrical, mechanical, and control-related issues to minimize production downtime.

· Install, test, and repair wiring, conduit, breakers, sensors, VFDs, PLC-connected equipment, and related electrical components.

· Read and interpret electrical schematics, blueprints, ladder logic, and technical manuals

· Support equipment installations, system upgrades, and continuous improvement initiatives throughout the facility.

· Respond quickly to equipment failures and emergency maintenance situations to support uninterrupted plant operations.

· Maintain accurate maintenance documentation, work orders, and repair records within all systems.

· Partner with production, sanitation, quality, and engineering teams to support operational goals and food safety standards.

· Ensure compliance with all GMP, SQF, HACCP, OSHA, and company safety standards.

· Perform electrical work in refrigerated, freezer, and production environments as needed.

· Participate in on-call rotations and flexible scheduling to support plant operations.

· Travel between and support multiple production facilities as needed, applying technical expertise to improve processes, solve problems, and drive continuous improvement across both Mariposa and Imperial.

· Other duties as assigned or necessary.



What You’ll Need:

· High school diploma or GED preferred; technical school certification or associate degree in Electrical Technology, Industrial Maintenance, or a related field strongly preferred.

· Valid Journeyman Electrician license required.

· 10+ years of industrial electrical maintenance experience within food manufacturing, frozen food, beverage, dairy, or other CPG manufacturing environments required.

· Strong knowledge of industrial electrical systems, motor controls, PLCs, VFDs, automation systems, and troubleshooting techniques.

· Experience working with 480V 3-phase electrical systems and industrial production equipment required.

· Ability to travel between and support multiple production facilities as needed.

· Ability to read and interpret electrical schematics, blueprints, and technical documentation.

· Familiarity with preventative maintenance programs.

· Strong understanding of OSHA, NFPA 70E, lockout/tagout, and electrical safety procedures.

· Strong troubleshooting and problem-solving skills with the ability to work independently with minimal supervision.

· Ability to work in cold, refrigerated, and freezer environments.

· Flexibility to work overtime, weekends, holidays, and on-call schedules as needed.
